heading: Enhanced authority for selective early discharges
label: 580a Enhanced authority for selective early discharges
notes: Amendments2015—Subsec. (a). , substituted “, and ending on ” for “, and ending on ”.Pub. L. 114–92, § 501(1)October 1, 2015October 1, 2019November 30, 1993October 1, 1999Subsec. (3) which read as follows: “The total number of regular warrant officers described in subsection (b) from any of the armed forces (or from any of the armed forces in a particular grade) who may be recommended during a fiscal year for discharge by a selection board convened pursuant to the authority of that subsection may not exceed 70 percent of the decrease, as compared to the preceding fiscal year, in the number of warrant officers of that armed force (or the number of warrant officers of that armed force in that grade) authorized to be serving on active duty as of the end of that fiscal year.”Pub. children.label: a
children.text: The Secretary of Defense may authorize the Secretary of a military department, during the period beginning on , and ending on , to take the action set forth in subsection (b) with respect to regular warrant officers of an armed force under the jurisdiction of that Secretary.October 1, 2015October 1, 2019
who have served at least one year of active duty in the grade currently held;
whose names are not on a list of warrant officers recommended for promotion; and
who are not eligible to be retired under any provision of law and are not within two years of becoming so eligible.

children.label: A
children.text: the names of all regular warrant officers described in that subsection in a particular grade and competitive category; or

children.label: B
children.text: the names of all regular warrant officers described in that subsection in a particular grade and competitive category who also are in particular year groups or specialties, or both, within that competitive category.

children.label: A
children.text: in each grade in each competitive category; or

children.label: B
children.text: in each grade, year group, or specialty (or combination thereof) in each competitive category.

A warrant officer who is recommended for discharge by a selection board convened pursuant to subsection (b) and whose discharge is approved by the Secretary concerned shall be discharged on a date specified by the Secretary concerned.
Selection of warrant officers for discharge under this subsection shall be based on the needs of the service.

children.label: d
children.text: The discharge of any warrant officer pursuant to this section shall be considered involuntary for purposes of any other provision of law.

children.label: e
children.text: This section applies to the Secretary of Homeland Security in the same manner and to the same extent as it applies to the Secretary of Defense. The Commandant of the Coast Guard shall take the action set forth in subsection (b) with respect to regular warrant officers of the Coast Guard.
